classifications.phrase: inflationary pressures are easing a little more quickly than expected
inflation is moving sustainably towards the midpoint of the 2–3 per cent target range
labour market may be somewhat tighter than previously thought
central forecast for underlying inflation has been revised up a little over 2026
Board remains cautious on prospects for further policy easing
Growth in output has been weak
growth in unit labour costs remains high
disinflation might be occurring a little more quickly than earlier expected
if monetary policy is eased too much too soon, disinflation could stall

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a cautious easing stance, acknowledging disinflation progress while remaining concerned about labour market tightness, upside inflation risks, and the dangers of premature policy relaxation.
